channel.title: Global Investing FX Terminal — AI Market Narrative
channel.description: AI-generated FX market narrative and signals, updated at each major session transition.

item.title: [MIXED] Strait of Hormuz tensions drive a geopolitical risk premium as oil surges tow…
item.description:
Strait of Hormuz tensions drive a geopolitical risk premium as oil surges toward $85 — USD/JPY holds at 162.33 with 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustaining the bid despite a strong -1.58 options put skew; gold higher +0.54%; equities -0.79%; DXY steady -0.05%; VIX 17.2. Watch US CPI at 12:30 UTC and BoC Rate Decision tomorrow.
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk: NZD/CHF at 0.4706 — G10 scorecard leader NZD (+0.664%) vs laggard CHF (-0.812%) creates a 1.476% spread, the widest in the G10 complex. Desp • USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Carry: USD/JPY at 162.33 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but institutional positioning signals exhaustion. COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% • USD/CAD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Oil Shock: USD/CAD at 1.4146 — Institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. COT LF net short -87,846 (22.6% long) shows specs are heavily short, whil

item.title: [MIXED] RBNZ rate hike bets firm as Governor Conway warns of persistent oil-driven in…
item.description:
RBNZ rate hike bets firm as Governor Conway warns of persistent oil-driven inflation, lifting NZD/USD to 0.5758; hawkish divergence builds as NZD real rates at -0.58% face further tightening. USD/JPY holds 162.44 on 275bp Fed-BoJ carry, though options signal caution with a -1.58 risk reversal put skew. Gold higher +0.06% near $4,004; equities -0.79%; DXY +0.04%; VIX 17.2. Watch US CPI and BoC Policy Report at 14:45 UTC.
Key signals: • NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ: RBNZ 2.5%. RBNZ's Conway warns oil-driven inflation is persistent, fueling rate hike bets. Crowded spec shorts face significant unwind risk • USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry: USD/CHF at 0.8147 driven by a 1.115% G10 leader-to-laggard spread. USD outperformance (avg +0.338%) vs CHF weakness (avg -0.777%) is support • USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Carry: USD/JPY at 162.44 holding above the critical 160.00 intervention threshold. +275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but 25d RR at -1.58 (Stro

item.title: [CAUTION] U.S. blockade on Iran and Hormuz shipping tolls drive a 9% crude surge, intro…
item.description:
U.S. blockade on Iran and Hormuz shipping tolls drive a 9% crude surge, introducing a sharp geopolitical risk premium; USD/JPY firms to 162.46 as hawkish Fed commentary offsets the 275bp carry-unwind risk. Gold lower -2.60% near $4,007; equities -0.79%; DXY +0.36%; VIX 17.2. USD/CHF IV at 24.8% vs HV30 6.3% signals extreme hedging demand. Watch BoC Rate Decision 13:45 UTC Wednesday.
Key signals: • USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Options Skew: USD/JPY at 162.46 — Trump's 20% Hormuz shipping fee and Iran blockade sent WTI oil surging 9.48%, driving a bear flattener regime in US yiel • USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Vol Risk Premium: USD/CHF at 0.8147 — G10 scorecard identifies a significant bilateral divergence as USD outperforms (+0.369%) while CHF lags (-0.726%). The 1 • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence: USD/CAD at 1.4156 — The historical inverse correlation between CAD and Oil has suffered a major break (corr -0.898 vs norm +0.006, z=-2.02σ)

item.title: [MIXED] Middle East hostilities drive a geopolitical risk premium as crude surges tow…
item.description:
Middle East hostilities drive a geopolitical risk premium as crude surges toward $75 — USD/JPY firms to 162.44 on a +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, though a strong -1.58 options put skew (25d RR) signals rising downside hedging; gold lower -2.67% near $4,004; equities -0.68%; DXY +0.29%; VIX 16.8. CAD outperforms G10 peers as Governor Macklem's MPR press conference looms; USD/CAD holds 1.4151. Watch BoC Rate Decision and MPR at 14:00 UTC Wednesday.
Key signals: • USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Options Skew: USD/JPY at 162.44 — Middle East hostilities and a +5.24% surge in WTI oil prices drive USD safe-haven demand, overriding traditional JPY hav • USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Volatility Premium: USD/CHF at 0.8138 — G10 scorecard identifies a significant bilateral divergence as USD outperforms (+0.286%) and CHF lags (-0.666%). The 375 • USD/CAD — Oil Correlation Break + Retail Divergence: USD/CAD at 1.4151 — The pair's correlation with WTI oil has collapsed to -0.899 (z = -2.02σ), as CAD fails to capture the +5.24% oil rally d

item.title: [MIXED] Middle East escalation and Hormuz transit risks drive a geopolitical risk pre…
item.description:
Middle East escalation and Hormuz transit risks drive a geopolitical risk premium — USD/JPY holds at 162.01 as 275bp Fed-BoJ carry offsets risk-premium demand; gold lower -0.81% near $4,080; equities +0.42%; DXY -0.03%; VIX 16.1. USD/JPY options show a moderate put skew (25d RR -1.38) as the market pays for protection against carry unwinds despite the spot bid. Watch BoC Rate Decision July 15 for the next G10 policy catalyst.
Key signals: • USD/JPY — Geopolitics + Carry + Options Skew: USD/JPY trades at 162.010, holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where BoJ intervention risk historically escalates. Despite escalatin • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning + Retail Divergence: USD/CAD trades at 1.41410, exhibiting a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI Crude Oil. The rolling correlation stands at -0.899 • NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence: NZD/USD trades at 0.57830, presenting a clear divergence between speculative positioning and central bank policy bias. RBNZ 2.5%. This creat

item.title: [MIXED] Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz following US strikes, introducing a geopolit…
item.description:
Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz following US strikes, introducing a geopolitical risk premium as WTI surges +4.19%; gold lower -0.79%; equities +0.42%; DXY +0.18%; VIX 15.0. USD/JPY holds at 162.03 as 275bp Fed-BoJ carry offsets risk-premium demand, though options show a moderate put skew with 25d RR at -1.38. USD/CAD at 1.4164 remains decoupled from crude (z=-2.02σ) ahead of the BoC meeting. Watch BoC Rate Decision July 15 and US CPI tomorrow.
Key signals: • USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Vol: USD/JPY at 162.03 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 (crowded c • NZD/USD — COT vs CB Divergence + RBNZ: NZD/USD at 0.5766 — COT LF net short -27,675 (specs fading the kiwi) contradicts the RBNZ's hawkish shift and 'Hike' market bias. With a -0. • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning + Retail: USD/CAD at 1.4164 — 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage and crowded COT LF net short -87,846 (long%=22.6%) keep the pair bid. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil c

item.title: [CAUTION] Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz dominates the weekend tape, setting up…
item.description:
Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz dominates the weekend tape, setting up a significant crude-driven risk-off bid for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1419; the geopolitical escalation and upcoming US CPI is likely to weigh on the pair as safe-haven USD demand likely intensifies. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.67; while JPY may see haven flows, the +255bp OIS carry advantage and hawkish Fed commentary from Kevin Warsh would support the pair at Sunday's open. NZD/CHF remains a critical bilateral theme following the RBNZ's first hike in three years (NZD 1W avg: +1.331%, CHF 1W avg: -0.710%, G10 spread: 2.041%).
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Policy Pivot: NZD 1W momentum (+1.331%) vs CHF laggard status (-0.710%) creates a 2.041% G10 spread following the RBNZ's first rate hike in three years. T • USD/JPY — Carry Advantage vs Speculative Squeeze Risk: JPY COT LF net short -104.2k (29.1% long) creates significant short-squeeze risk despite the +255bp OIS carry advantage favoring USD. While • EUR/USD — Political Risk + Negative Data Momentum: EUR/USD downside toward 1.1350 is driven by the death of Senator Lindsey Graham and potential US fiscal uncertainty alongside a negative EUR

item.title: [MIXED] The RBNZ’s first rate hike in three years dominates the weekend tape, setting…
item.description:
The RBNZ’s first rate hike in three years dominates the weekend tape, setting up NZD for outperformance at the Tokyo open. NZD/USD closed Friday at 0.5763; the hawkish pivot likely sustains the NZD 1W avg lead of +1.331% against the laggard CHF (Friday close: 0.8078 for USD/CHF). While Iran-driven oil volatility is likely to support CAD at the open, upcoming US CPI and Warsh testimony would likely provide a geopolitical risk premium for USD, pressuring EUR/USD from its Friday close of 1.1419.
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme: NZD outperformance accelerates following the RBNZ's first rate hike in three years, while CHF remains the G10 laggard with a -0.710% weekly • USD/JPY — COT Short Squeeze Risk + US CPI Catalyst: JPY COT net short -104,231 (29.1% long) creates massive squeeze risk for USD/JPY if upcoming US CPI data underwhelms. While the +255bp OIS c • USD/CAD — COT Crowding + BoC Policy Decision: CAD COT net short -87,846 (22.6% long) and an 80% retail short bias on USD/CAD signal a heavily one-sided market ahead of Wednesday's rate d

item.title: [MIXED] Trump ceasefire headlines and Middle East uncertainty dominate the weekend ta…
item.description:
Trump ceasefire headlines and Middle East uncertainty dominate the weekend tape, likely to test USD risk-premium demand at the Tokyo open. GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3398; risk appetite is likely to support the pair given the +22 CESI beat. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.67; a bear steepener regime and +260bp OIS carry advantage would support the pair at Sunday's open. Fed tariff warnings and RBNZ hike bias set up NZD/USD for outperformance at the open.
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme: NZD 1W momentum (+1.403%) vs CHF laggard status (-0.795%) creates a 2.198% G10 spread that favors upside continuation. The RBNZ hiking bias • USD/JPY — Fed Inflation Warning + Carry Advantage: JPY COT net short -137.8k (74.7% short) remains the dominant G10 positioning extreme, leaving the pair vulnerable to a squeeze if the 161.67 • AUD/USD — China Reflation Gap + Carry Support: AUD COT net long +21.5k (62.1% long) aligns with a +77bp OIS carry advantage to support the 0.6955 Friday close. Slowing Chinese CPI at 1.0%

item.title: [MIXED] Middle East tensions and US-Iran policy uncertainty drive the session's risk …
item.description:
Middle East tensions and US-Iran policy uncertainty drive the session's risk premium, though markets largely shrug off claims that the ceasefire has ended. USD/JPY retreats to 161.36 as Japan urges pension funds to invest in domestic assets, while the 275bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry limit downside. Gold lower -0.53% near $4,119; equities +0.21%; DXY -0.13%; VIX 15.4. Watch the BoC rate decision on July 15 as the next key policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme: NZD/CHF at 0.7165 (implied) — NZD is the week's G10 leader (+1.480%) while CHF laggard status (-0.739%) creates a 2.218% spread. Despite NZD • USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY at 161.36 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological level previously associated with BoJ intervention warnings. JPY COT LF net sh • AUD/USD — Volatility + Positioning Divergence: AUD/USD at 0.6967 — Options market pricing reflects extreme caution with IV at 30.7% vs HV30 of 7.0%, a +23.7% VRP. This massive vol premium

item.title: [MIXED] Japan producer prices hit their fastest pace since 2023, firming BoJ hike exp…
item.description:
Japan producer prices hit their fastest pace since 2023, firming BoJ hike expectations as USD/JPY retreats to 161.83; gold lower -0.69% near $4,112; equities +0.81%; DXY -0.04%; VIX 16.0. JPY gains follow FinMin Katayama's warnings on policy shifts, while USD/JPY options show a moderate put skew with a 3.6pt vol risk premium. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% maintains a 275bp carry advantage for USD, but rising real rates in Japan challenge the carry trade. Watch Canada Jobs data at 12:30 UTC.
Key signals: • NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme: NZD/CHF at 0.7156 — NZD leads G10 performance (+1.509%) while CHF lags (-0.651%), creating a 2.160% spread. The divergence is fundamentally • USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY at 161.83 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but price is now testing levels above the 160.00 threshold where BoJ verbal warnin • USD/CAD — Positioning + CESI + Retail Divergence: USD/CAD at 1.4166 — COT LF net short -92,861 (20.6% long) aligns with a negative CESI (-8), signaling persistent CAD data disappointment. Re

item.title: [MIXED] Dollar retreats as global equities rally and crude oil consolidates, driving …
item.description:
Dollar retreats as global equities rally and crude oil consolidates, driving DXY lower -0.28% while gold holds near $4,136 -0.10% on US-Iran conflict assessment. The risk-on tone lifts GBP/USD to 1.3446 and EUR/USD to 1.1461, supported by rising ECB and BoE rate hike bets that challenge the Fed's +150bp nominal carry advantage. USD/JPY falls to 161.446 as Japan's producer prices hit their fastest pace since 2023, keeping the BoJ on track to tighten; options markets show a moderate put skew with USD/JPY 25d RR at -1.28. Equities rise +0.81% and the VIX consolidates 15.8. Watch the BoC rate decision on July 15 as the next key policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme: NZD/USD trades at 0.5791 as the G10 currency scorecard triggers a critical bilateral theme. NZD outperformance is driven by the RBNZ hiking • USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY trades at 161.446, holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where verbal and physical BoJ intervention risks rise. JPY speculativ • GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence: GBP/USD trades at 1.34460, supported by a positive economic surprise index (GBP CESI net +23 beats) and a neutral carry profile (Fed 3.75% v

item.title: [MIXED] The dollar retreats as equities rally and crude oil falls, while the Federal …
item.description:
The dollar retreats as equities rally and crude oil falls, while the Federal Reserve unveils its Warsh task forces to lead a broad policy review. USD/JPY consolidates at 162.35, where the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage continues to anchor the pair despite a moderate put skew in the 25d RR (Saxo, 1M, indicative mid) at -1.28. Cross-asset flows show gold higher +1.23%; equities +0.81%; DXY -0.06%; VIX 15.8. Watch the ECB policy decision on July 23 as the next major G10 catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence: NZD/JPY spot at 112.591 is the focal point of a massive G10 divergence theme as NZD outperformance collides with JPY weakness. The RBNZ is i • USD/JPY — Positioning + Volatility + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY trades at 162.349, holding well above the critical 160.00 psychological threshold where BoJ verbal and physical intervention risks e • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning Divergence: USD/CAD trades at 1.4165, showing a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI crude oil. The rolling correlation has collapsed to -0.

item.title: [MIXED] RBNZ on hold at 2.5%. The broader US Dollar retreats as Middle East calm cool…
item.description:
RBNZ on hold at 2.5%. The broader US Dollar retreats as Middle East calm cools inflation fears, with gold higher +1.51% near $4,144, equities +0.64%, DXY -0.12%, and VIX 16.0. Watch the ECB rate decision on July 23 as the next major G10 policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hawkish Hike: NZD/JPY surges as the RBNZ delivers a hawkish rate hike to 2.5% (HIKING), driving NZD to a three-week high of 0.5763 against the USD. This m • USD/JPY — Carry Dominance vs Intervention Risk at 162.28: USD/JPY trades at 162.28, holding well above the critical 160.00 psychological threshold that previously triggered verbal intervention warni • AUD/USD — Extreme Options Hedging + COT Long Divergence: AUD/USD trades at 0.6945, supported by a −73bp OIS carry premium favoring AUD (SOFR 3.62% vs AONIA 4.35%). Speculative positioning is net lo

item.title: [MIXED] New Zealand Dollar firms above 0.5737 as RBNZ signals further policy tighteni…
item.description:
New Zealand Dollar firms above 0.5737 as RBNZ signals further policy tightening, anchoring a dominant G10 outperformance theme where NZD leads the weekly scorecard (+0.977% avg, n=4) against a weakening JPY (-0.804% avg, n=7) with a 1.782% spread. The 250bp NZD/CHF OIS carry advantage supports the kiwi bid, while gold rises +0.79% to $4,115, equities retreat -0.28%, DXY consolidates -0.02%, and VIX holds at 16.9. Watch the ECB rate decision on July 23 for the next major G10 policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hawkish Bias: NZD/JPY trades at 93.19 as the G10 currency strength scorecard triggers a critical bilateral theme. NZD is the strongest G10 currency this w • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Sentiment Divergence: USD/CAD trades at 1.4179, exhibiting a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI Oil. The rolling correlation stands at -0.888 vs a 2 • GBP/USD — Volatility Risk Premium + Retail Divergence: GBP/USD trades at 1.3412, supported by a positive Economic Surprise Index (CESI net +23 beats) and a mild OIS carry advantage (SONIA 3.73% v

item.title: [MIXED] Fed on hold at 3.75%. The risk premium and a +275bp OIS carry advantage keep …
item.description:
Fed on hold at 3.75%. The risk premium and a +275bp OIS carry advantage keep USD/JPY bid at 162.46, while options price a notable premium with USD/JPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 3.3%. Broad risk-off sentiment remains contained as equities consolidate -0.28%, DXY holds -0.02%, and VIX prints 16.9. Watch the Bank of Canada rate decision on July 15 as the next key policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence: NZD/JPY trades at 93.00 as the G10 Currency Strength Scorecard triggers a critical bilateral theme. NZD is the strongest G10 currency this w • USD/JPY — FOMC Minutes + Options Skew Divergence: USD/JPY trades at 162.46, holding above the critical 160.00 BoJ intervention threshold. The June FOMC minutes revealed some officials were r • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Volatility Premium: USD/CAD trades at 1.4164, exhibiting a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI crude oil. item.title: [MIXED] Trump declaring the U.S.-Iran ceasefire over drives crude higher, introducing…
item.description:
Trump declaring the U.S.-Iran ceasefire over drives crude higher, introducing a geopolitical risk premium as USD/JPY tests 162.57; the 275bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry sustains the pair's bid despite verbal intervention risk. June FOMC minutes lean hawkish, confirming a higher-for-longer bias that supports USD. Gold lower -1.72%; equities -0.28%; DXY -0.01%; VIX 16.9. Watch the ECB policy meeting on July 23.
Key signals: • GBP/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry: GBP/JPY trades at 217.687 as the G10 currency strength scorecard triggers a critical-level bilateral theme. GBP leads G10 performance with a • USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY trades at 162.572, holding well above the critical 160.00 psychological threshold. The +275bp SOFR-TONA OIS carry premium continues • USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning: USD/CAD trades at 1.4166, exhibiting a severe cross-asset correlation break. The rolling USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has collapsed to -0.

item.title: [CAUTION] Middle East tensions and a sharp crude oil supply shock drive global risk-pre…
item.description:
Middle East tensions and a sharp crude oil supply shock drive global risk-premium repricing, lifting the dollar as equities slide and WTI surges over 7% — gold lower -2.71%; equities -0.77%; DXY +0.21%; VIX 18.3. The oil shock severely pressures the energy-importing yen, pushing USD/JPY to 162.64 as the 275bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry advantage remains highly insulated. GBP/USD holds firm at 1.3363, supported by a positive CESI (+24 beats) and a neutral 0bp Fed-BoE carry spread. Watch the June FOMC Minutes at 18:00 UTC for policy clues.
Key signals: • GBP/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Middle East Oil Shock: GBP/JPY trades at 217.344, driven by a widening G10 performance spread where GBP leads (+0.594% 1W avg) and JPY lags (-0.621% 1W avg). The M • USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk: USD/JPY trades at 162.642, holding well above the critical 160.00 level that has previously triggered Bank of Japan verbal and physical inte • USD/CAD vs WTI Oil — Cross-Asset Correlation Break: The rolling correlation between USD/CAD and WTI crude oil has collapsed to corr=-0.884, representing a -2.01σ deviation from its 252-day his

item.title: [MIXED] Trump's termination of the Iran memorandum at the NATO summit drives geopolit…
item.description:
Trump's termination of the Iran memorandum at the NATO summit drives geopolitical risk premiums, sending WTI crude +5.64% higher to $74.4 and lifting CAD. USD/CAD retreats to 1.4162 as oil gains, while USD/JPY consolidates at 162.478 under the weight of a strong put skew with 25d RR at -1.52. Cross-asset risk-off flows remain marginal under a mixed regime: gold lower -1.78%; equities -0.45%; DXY +0.06%; VIX 18.4. Watch the RBNZ rate decision at 02:00 UTC tomorrow.
Key signals: • USD/JPY — Geopolitical Escalation + Options Skew + Carry: USD/JPY trades at 162.48, holding above the 162.40 resistance area as US President Trump declares the Iran Memorandum of Understanding over • CAD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Oil Supply Shock: CAD/JPY trades at 114.719, driven by a prominent G10 bilateral theme where CAD is the weekly leader and JPY is the laggard, generating a 1.0 • NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Hike Bias: NZD/USD trades at 0.5701 following the RBNZ policy decision where the central bank raised its policy rate to 2.25%. A stark divergence exist

item.title: [MIXED] Fresh US military strikes on Iran and the RBNZ's hawkish 25bp rate hike to 2.…
item.description:
Fresh US military strikes on Iran and the RBNZ's hawkish 25bp rate hike to 2.50% dominate the session — USD/JPY firms to 162.38 on a +275bp OIS carry advantage, while NZD/USD bids to 0.5697. The geopolitical escalation supports the dollar as gold falls -0.89%; equities retreat -0.45%; DXY consolidates +0.13%; VIX rises 16.1. Watch the FOMC Minutes at 18:00 UTC for the next major policy catalyst.
Key signals: • NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Policy Shock: The RBNZ raised the Official Cash Rate by 25 bps to 2.50% and flagged further hikes to combat persistent inflation risks. This hawkish guida • AUD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence: AUD/JPY is the primary vehicle for this week's dominant G10 bilateral theme, with AUD outperforming as the weekly leader (+0.653% avg across • USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Geopolitical Risk: USD/JPY trades at 162.379, holding well above the critical 160.00 level that previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings, elevating unilateral
